Title: A signaling pathway mediating abscisic acid-induced jasmonic acid biosynthesis during strawberry ripening

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: jasmonic acid; methyl jasmonate; JA-Ile; ester aroma compounds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes fruit ripening and aroma production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ABA-induced jasmonate accumulation and aroma production; knockout inhibits both
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; ABA treatment; jasmonate quantification by HPLC-MS/MS; enzyme activity assays; gene expression analysis; BiFC; Co-IP; IP-kinase assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSuper1300/GFP overexpression construct (constitutive/super promoter context for transient/stable assays); native FvJAZ12 promoter used in complementation assays of downstream component
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK2 serine/threonine protein kinase; ABA signaling component; OST1 homolog
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous ABA treatment; phenidone inhibitor assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvSnRK2.6 physically interacts with FvABI1 and is activated by ABA, confirming it as an ABA signaling component. 2. Overexpression of FvSnRK2.6 increases jasmonate accumulation, LOX/AAT activity, and aroma-related gene expression, whereas CRISPR knockout reduces these traits. 3. FvSnRK2.6 phosphorylates FvJAZ12 and promotes its degradation, linking ABA signaling to JA biosynthesis and aroma formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving strawberry aroma via tuning ABA-JA crosstalk; should be deployed with caution because it is a central ABA signaling component and may affect broader stress/development traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a kinase, FvSnRK2.6 was experimentally positioned as a signaling component rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to produce hydroperoxy fatty acids that enter jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts as a key JA biosynthetic gene positively regulated by FvMYC2 in ripening fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression of FvMYC2; EMSA; effector-reporter GUS assay; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in GUS reporter assay; Super promoter for FvMYC2 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvLOX3 is one of the core jasmonate biosynthetic genes induced by ABA. 2. FvMYC2 binds the FvLOX3 promoter and activates its expression. 3. FvLOX3 is part of the downstream module through which the ABA-FvSnRK2.6-FvJAZ12-FvMYC2 cascade stimulates jasmonate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream pathway gene for boosting JA-associated aroma traits, potentially in combination with upstream regulatory components.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also discussed previously as part of the FvJAZ12-FvMYC2-FvLOX3 flavor pathway.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively contributes to JA biosynthesis as a direct transcriptional target of FvMYC2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression of FvMYC2; EMSA; effector-reporter GUS assay; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in GUS reporter assay; Super promoter for FvMYC2 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvAOC3 expression increases upon FvMYC2 overexpression. 2. FvMYC2 directly binds the FvAOC3 promoter and activates reporter expression. 3. FvAOC3 functions in the downstream JA biosynthetic module activated by ABA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking target with other JA biosynthetic genes to enhance aroma-associated jasmonate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct mutant analysis for FvAOC3 itself in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively contributes to JA biosynthesis as a direct transcriptional target of FvMYC2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression of FvMYC2; EMSA; effector-reporter GUS assay; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in GUS reporter assay; Super promoter for FvMYC2 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvAOS is a key JA pathway gene induced downstream of ABA signaling. 2. FvMYC2 directly binds to and activates the FvAOS promoter. 3. FvAOS is part of the final transcriptional output of the ABA-FvSnRK2.6-FvJAZ12-FvMYC2 cascade.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway engineering target to strengthen JA-linked ripening aroma when coordinated with other pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzyme manipulation was not performed in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively contributes to JA biosynthesis as a direct transcriptional target of FvMYC2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression of FvMYC2; EMSA; effector-reporter GUS assay; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in GUS reporter assay; Super promoter for FvMYC2 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvOPR3 transcript is elevated by FvMYC2 overexpression. 2. FvMYC2 directly binds and transcriptionally activates the FvOPR3 promoter. 3. FvOPR3 is a downstream effector gene in the ABA-induced JA biosynthetic cascade.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downstream pathway target for metabolic engineering of jasmonate-supported aroma traits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as one of four principal JA biosynthetic genes tested as direct FvMYC2 targets.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: jasmonic acid; methyl jasmonate; JA-Ile; ester aroma compounds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: jasmonates promote aroma production during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via binding/inhibiting FvMYC2; protein stability control by phosphorylation-dependent degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses FvMYC2, thereby indirectly repressing FvLOX3; FvAOC3; FvAOS; FvOPR3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: critical ABA-responsive repressor whose phosphorylation and degradation are required for full ABA-induced jasmonate and aroma production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FvJAZ12 represses JA biosynthesis and aroma production; phosphorylation at S142 by FvSnRK2.6 promotes its degradation and relieves repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC family screening; Co-IP; luciferase complementation; in vitro kinase assay; IP-kinase assay; Phos-tag assay; cell-free degradation assay; stable transgenics; complementation with WT and S142A mutant; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functionally analyzed in ripening fruit under ABA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native FvJAZ12 promoter in complementation assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering phosphorylation-sensitive or expression-tuned alleles may modulate strawberry aroma and JA levels; non-phosphorylatable forms suppress desired aroma output.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stabilized repressor allele (S142A) lowers JA accumulation and ester aroma production
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment; MG132 in degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvJAZ12 is the only tested FvJAZ member that physically interacts with FvSnRK2.6. 2. FvSnRK2.6 phosphorylates FvJAZ12 at S142 in the JAS domain in response to ABA, promoting ubiquitination-associated degradation. 3. The non-phosphorylatable FvJAZ12S142A allele fails to restore ABA-induced JA biosynthesis gene expression, jasmonate accumulation, and ester aroma production, showing that S142 phosphorylation is functionally essential.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work also showed phosphorylation by FvMAPK6 at different sites (S179/T183), indicating multi-layer regulation of FvJAZ12.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes aroma production during strawberry ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates promoter activity and transcript accumulation of FvLOX3, FvAOC3, FvAOS, and FvOPR3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator connecting relief of JAZ repression to transcription of core JA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: repression by FvJAZ12; ABA indirectly relieves this repression through FvSnRK2.6-mediated FvJAZ12 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates jasmonate biosynthesis genes and thereby promotes JA accumulation and downstream aroma formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; EMSA; effector-reporter GUS assays; luciferase complementation imaging
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Super promoter for effector construct; native promoters of FvLOX3/FvAOC3/FvAOS/FvOPR3 in reporters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for boosting JA-dependent aroma, especially in combination with reduced JAZ repression or tuned ABA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment in interaction and reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvMYC2 directly binds promoters of FvLOX3, FvAOC3, FvAOS, and FvOPR3. 2. FvMYC2 overexpression increases transcription of these JA biosynthetic genes. 3. ABA reduces FvJAZ12-FvMYC2 interaction, allowing FvMYC2 to activate the JA biosynthetic program.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper positions FvMYC2 as the immediate transcriptional activator downstream of FvJAZ12 in the ABA-to-JA cascade.
